calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Vacated

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Apr-16-2024 Off Calendar

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Apr-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
David Derderian, Esq. QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271 dderderian@quillarrowlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff, RODNEY MIRASOL
Kelli A. Herzog, Esq., GROTEFELD HOFFMANN LLP 415.344.9670 kherzog@ghlaw-llp.com Attorneys for Defendant AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Matter is not contested. Counsel seeks clarification of the Court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RODNEY MIRASOL MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

plaintiff's motion to compel further responses by Honda to the Second Set of Requests for Production of Documents (RPD) is GRANTED, in part. Honda is ordered to serve supplementary/amended responses to the RPD in accordance with a scope of response limited to California but including information/documents on substantially similar nonconformities/defects in vehicles of the same year and model as plaintiff's. The same production of SBA, recall and restitution/replacement policies mandated by the Los Angeles general orders (Exhibits A and B to plaintiff's January 29, 2024 meet and confer letter) are to produced by Honda in this action. Any objections included in Honda's supplementary/amended responses shall be tailored to the specific request item. No objections (blunderbuss or otherwise) shall be offered without the specificity required by the governing provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure. Given the mixed results of this order, plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a proposed order in accordance with the foregoing in compliance with Rule 3.1312 of the Rules of Court. Once counsel for defendant has approved the order as to form, it should be forwarded to psw@hassard.com for signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
